25.definition: To love, to be fond of, to love dearly. Used of the highest kind of love, divine love. This is the supreme Greek word for love, expressing the most noble form of affection.

etymology.historicalNote: Common in Koine Greek for divine and Christian love (agape). Appears 143 times in the NT. Central to Christian ethics and theology.

2316.definition: God. The divine being, the supreme ruler and creator of all things. In the NT, this refers specifically to the God of Israel and the Father of Jesus Christ.

etymology.historicalNote: The most important theological term in the NT. Appears 1,320 times.

4100.definition: To believe, to trust, to have confidence in. Often followed by εἰς (into) with accusative. In the NT, it frequently means to trust in Christ for salvation.

etymology.historicalNote: Key concept in NT soteriology. Represents faith and trust in God/Christ. The noun πίστις appears 243 times.
